title: Kalanick Returns to the Table with Atoms: a16z Leads $1.7 Billion Uber Investment
excerpt: Travis Kalanick is back, and this time, he's being brought back with real money. The robotics company founded by the Uber co-founder, Atoms, has just closed a $
content_html:
Travis Kalanick is back, and this time, he's being brought back with real money. The robotics company founded by the Uber co-founder, Atoms, has just closed a $1.7 billion funding round led by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), with a16z co-founder Ben Horowitz also joining the board of Atoms. Bain Capital, Fifth Wall and other institutions also participated as co-investors.

The most intriguing detail is that Uber also appears on the list of investors in this round. This reconnects Kalanick with the company he founded, which he was forced to step down from as CEO in 2017 due to allegations of sexual harassment and discrimination, as well as a toxic workplace culture.

Atoms is essentially a rebranded holding company, with its foundation being the project Kalanick has been working on since leaving Uber — a ghost kitchen business called Cloud Kitchens. When he unveiled the new name this March, he announced that he had acquired Pronto, an industrial automation company led by Anthony Levandowski, one of his former Uber colleagues. Kalanick also stated that he wanted to expand into the mining sector through Atoms, extending what Pronto currently does for vehicles in mines. Last year, there were reports that he intended to acquire the US operations of Chinese autonomous driving company Pony.ai with Uber's support, but a March report from The Information said the talks had fallen through.

In the X post announcing the funding, Kalanick did not elaborate on what Atoms exactly aims to build, only referring to this round as a "unfinished mission." He wrote that this fuel would be used to complete the story arc that started at Uber, continued with Cloud Kitchens, and now concludes with Atoms — the journey "from bits to atoms." Sixteen years ago, he began a journey to digitize the physical world — using software to understand, predict, and control the physical world, building a "computer based on atoms" with manufacturing as its CPU, real estate as its storage, and transportation as its network.

Kalanick said he wants Atoms to create a "platform for robots."

"Travis is back," said Horowitz, who posted almost simultaneously, with no attempt to hide his enthusiasm. He said that entrepreneurs who can move the old and heavy parts of the economy are extremely rare, requiring both grit and a broad span from software architecture to mechanical engineering, and Kalanick is such a person. Horowitz wrote that the entire point of Atoms is to make people more productive in the physical world — to repeat Uber's transformation of mobility and the computer's transformation of the digital world, making everything and everyone more productive.

Kalanick didn't specify details, but between the lines, it seems that a significant portion of this money will go toward hiring. He wrote that those who change the world will eventually face the ultimate boss — nature, and its fierce resistance to change; in the new industrial era, nature will do everything it can to hinder builders, and only the most resilient humans can push these complex systems and industries across the finish line.

sentences.en: He wrote that those who change the world will eventually face the ultimate boss — nature, and its fierce resistance to change; in the new industrial era, nature will do everything it can to hinder builders, and only the most resilient humans can push these complex systems and industries across the finish line.
sentences.zh: 他写道，那些改变世界的人最终将面对最终的老板——自然，以及它对改变的强烈抵制；在新工业时代，大自然会竭尽全力阻碍建设者，只有最具韧性的人类才能推动这些复杂的系统和产业冲过终点线。

content_html_zh:
特拉维斯·卡兰尼克回来了，这一次，他是用真金白银带回来的。 由 Uber 联合创始人 Atoms 创立的机器人公司刚刚完成了由 Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) 领投的 17 亿美元融资，a16z 联合创始人 Ben Horowitz 也加入了 Atoms 董事会。 贝恩资本、Fifth Wall等机构也作为联合投资者参与其中。

最耐人寻味的细节是，Uber也出现在本轮投资者名单中。 这让卡兰尼克与他创立的公司重新建立了联系。由于性骚扰和歧视指控以及有毒的工作场所文化，他于 2017 年被迫辞去首席执行官职务。

Atoms 本质上是一家重新命名的控股公司，其基础是卡兰尼克离开 Uber 后一直致力于的项目——一家名为 Cloud Kitchens 的幽灵厨房企业。 今年 3 月，当他公布新名称时，他宣布收购了 Pronto，这是一家由他的 Uber 前同事 Anthony Levandowski 领导的工业自动化公司。 卡兰尼克还表示，他希望通过 Atoms 进军采矿业，扩展 Pronto 目前在矿山车辆方面的业务。 去年，有报道称他打算在 Uber 的支持下收购中国自动驾驶公司小马智行的美国业务，但 The Information 3 月份的报道称谈判已告失败。

在宣布融资的 X 帖子中，卡兰尼克没有详细说明 Atoms 的具体目标是什么，只是将这一轮称为“未完成的任务”。 他写道，这种燃料将用于完成从 Uber 开始，到 Cloud Kitchens，现在到 Atoms 结束的故事线——“从比特到原子”的旅程。 十六年前，他开始了物理世界数字化之旅——用软件来理解、预测和控制物理世界，打造一台以制造业为CPU、房地产为存储、交通为网络的“基于原子的计算机”。

卡兰尼克表示，他希望 Atoms 创建一个“机器人平台”。

“特拉维斯回来了，”霍洛维茨说道，他几乎同时发帖，毫不掩饰他的热情。 他表示，能够转移经济中旧的、沉重的部分的企业家极其罕见，需要勇气和从软件架构到机械工程的广泛跨度，而卡兰尼克就是这样的人。 Horowitz 写道，Atoms 的全部意义在于让人们在现实世界中提高生产力——重复 Uber 对移动性的变革和计算机对数字世界的变革，让一切和每个人都更有生产力。

卡兰尼克没有具体说明细节，但言外之意是，这笔钱的很大一部分似乎将用于招聘。 他写道，那些改变世界的人最终将面对最终的老板——自然，以及它对改变的强烈抵制；在新工业时代，大自然会竭尽全力阻碍建设者，只有最具韧性的人类才能推动这些复杂的系统和产业冲过终点线。
